title: Nagaland Zoological Park
tagline: Nagaland Zoological Park — wildlife reserve of Nagaland

overview.short: The Nagaland Zoological Park is a 176-hectare (430-acre) zoo located at Rangapahar in the Chümoukedima District of Nagaland.
overview.description: The Nagaland Zoological Park is a 176-hectare (430-acre) zoo located at Rangapahar in the Chümoukedima District of Nagaland. It is Nagaland's biggest zoo and was opened to public in August 2008. The park is a part of the Rangapahar Wildlife Sanctuary.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Rangapahar Wildlife Sanctuary
topPlaces.description: Rangapahar Wildlife Sanctuary is a wildlife sanctuary in the Chümoukedima District of the Indian state of Nagaland. The sanctuary spans approximately 4.7 km² (470 hectares) and was notified as a wildlife sanctuary in 1986 to conserve remnant forest patches and native wildlife in the western Nagaland landscape.

topPlaces.name: Nagaland Cricket Association Stadium
topPlaces.description: The Nagaland Cricket Association Stadium or Sovima Cricket Stadium is a cricket stadium located at Sovima in the Chümoukedima District of Nagaland. It is the only cricket stadium in the northeastern Indian state of Nagaland.

topPlaces.name: Kuda, Dimapur
topPlaces.description: Kuda, formerly Nagarjan, is an Angami Naga village, located in the Dimapur District of the Indian state of Nagaland, India. Founded in 1941, Kuda is the oldest recognised Naga village in Dimapur area and one of the biggest in Nagaland.

topPlaces.name: Kirha
topPlaces.description: Kirha is a village located in the Chümoukedima District of Nagaland and is a suburb of Chümoukedima, the district headquarters.

topPlaces.name: Sovima
topPlaces.description: Sovima is an Angami Naga village located in Chümoukedima District of Nagaland, India and is a suburb of Chümoukedima, the district headquarter.

topPlaces.name: Chümoukedima metropolitan area
topPlaces.description: Chümoukedima Metropolitan Area is the metropolitan area of Chümoukedima and its surrounding region, located in south-west Nagaland, India. It is the largest metropolitan area in Nagaland and the third largest in population after Dimapur and Kohima.

topPlaces.name: Ruins of Kachari Rajbari
topPlaces.description: Ruins of Kachari Rajbari are a set of medieval monuments located in Dimapur, Nagaland, in Northeast India. Their history dates back to the 10th century when they appeared during the Kachari civilization.

topPlaces.name: Dimapur
topPlaces.description: Dimapur () is the largest city and municipality in the Indian state of Nagaland. As of 2024 , the municipality had a population of 172,000.

items.note: Rangapahar Wildlife Sanctuary is a wildlife sanctuary in the Chümoukedima District of the Indian state of Nagaland. The sanctuary spans approximately 4.7 km² (470 hectares) and was notified as a wildlife sanctuary in 1986 to conserve remnant forest patches and native wildlife in the western Nagaland landscape.

The Nagaland Cricket Association Stadium or Sovima Cricket Stadium is a cricket stadium located at Sovima in the Chümoukedima District of Nagaland. It is the only cricket stadium in the northeastern Indian state of Nagaland.

Kuda, formerly Nagarjan, is an Angami Naga village, located in the Dimapur District of the Indian state of Nagaland, India. Founded in 1941, Kuda is the oldest recognised Naga village in Dimapur area and one of the biggest in Nagaland.
Kirha is a village located in the Chümoukedima District of Nagaland and is a suburb of Chümoukedima, the district headquarters.
Sovima is an Angami Naga village located in Chümoukedima District of Nagaland, India and is a suburb of Chümoukedima, the district headquarter.

Chümoukedima Metropolitan Area is the metropolitan area of Chümoukedima and its surrounding region, located in south-west Nagaland, India. It is the largest metropolitan area in Nagaland and the third largest in population after Dimapur and Kohima.

Dimapur () is the largest city and municipality in the Indian state of Nagaland. As of 2024 , the municipality had a population of 172,000.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Nagaland Zoological Park?
faq.a: The best time to visit Nagaland Zoological Park is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Nagaland Zoological Park?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Dimapur Airport (6 km) and the nearest railway station is Dimapur Railway Station (6 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Nagaland Zoological Park?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Nagaland Zoological Park at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Nagaland Zoological Park?
faq.a: Stays in Nagaland Zoological Park start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Nagaland Zoological Park?
faq.a: The top attractions are Rangapahar Wildlife Sanctuary, Nagaland Cricket Association Stadium, Kuda, Dimapur, Kirha, Sovima.
